title: China 11-Day Itinerary
summary: Shanghai start, a Suzhou day trip, Fenghuang water town, Zhangjiajie peaks, then Beijing classics before flying south to Taipei.

stays.label: Homeinn Selected Hotel (Nanjing Road)
stays.address: No. 11, Lane 135, Jiangxi Middle Road, Huangpu District, Shanghai, 200002, China
stays.note: Walking distance to The Bund and Nanjing Road East. Use this base for the three Shanghai days and the Suzhou day trip.
stays.transit: Nanjing East Road Station (Lines 2 and 10) is a short walk. From PVG, take Metro Line 2 directly, or the Maglev to Longyang Road then Line 2.

stays.label: Tianqingshe Riverview Homestay
stays.address: No. 70 Jinping Road, Tuojiang Town, Fenghuang County, Hunan, China
stays.note: Riverside guesthouse inside the ancient town. Perfect for the lantern-lit evening walk along the Tuojiang River.
stays.transit: About 4 hours by car or shuttle from Zhangjiajie Hehua (DYG) Airport. Drop-off close to Hong Bridge.

stays.label: Zhinanzhen Boutique Inn
stays.address: Guanliping Resettlement Community, Yongding District, Zhangjiajie, Hunan, China
stays.note: Close to the park access roads. Use this base for the National Forest Park day and Tianmen Mountain.
stays.transit: About 30 to 45 minutes by taxi from Zhangjiajie Hehua Airport (DYG). Park shuttles connect to Wulingyuan and Tianmen.

stays.label: PiaoHome Chain Hotel (Guomao Donghua)
stays.address: No. 1-6, Building 4, Chaijiawan, Chaoyang District, Beijing, 100025, China
stays.note: Chaoyang base for the Beijing classics. Late check-in expected because the Zhangjiajie flight lands after midnight.
stays.transit: Closest stations are Sihui and Sihui East (Lines 1 and Batong). Quick taxi to CBD and Wangfujing. Daxing Airport is about 60 to 75 minutes by taxi.

days.label: Day 1
days.focus: Travel day. Long-haul to Pudong with one stop, lands the next morning.
days.transport_note: Pack light carry-on items for the layover. Eat and sleep on the second leg if you can.

days.label: Day 2
days.focus: Easy arrival day. Check in, walk to the Bund, and warm up on Nanjing Road East.
days.transport_note: Stay on foot once you check in. Hotel is steps from the Bund and Nanjing Road East.

start_transfer.title: PVG Airport to Hotel
start_transfer.instructions: From Pudong Airport, take Metro Line 2 toward downtown and exit at Nanjing East Road Station. The hotel is a short walk down Jiangxi Middle Road. The Maglev to Longyang Road then Line 2 is faster if you want to skip the long stretch.
start_transfer.note: Taxis from PVG run about 60 to 80 minutes in Friday traffic. Metro is the most reliable arrival option.

places.name: The Bund
places.summary: Riverside promenade lined with European-era facades on one side and the Pudong skyline across the water. The classic Shanghai postcard view.
places.transport: Walk from the hotel along Nanjing Road East to the waterfront. Or take Metro Line 2 to Nanjing East Road Station.

foods.name: Shengjianbao
foods.description: Pan-fried pork buns with a crisp golden bottom, soft top, and a small burst of broth inside.
foods.where: Yang's Fry Dumplings (Xiaoyang Shengjian) on Wujiang Road or the Huanghe Road branch.

places.name: Nanjing Road East
places.summary: Pedestrian shopping street that links People's Square to the Bund. Bright signs, snack stalls, and a good jet-lag stroll.
highlights: Old department stores and neon signs
places.transport: Walk from the hotel. The street begins one block east at Henan Middle Road and runs all the way to the Bund.

images.title: File:2024-Apr Shanghai East Nanjing Road morning 01.jpg

transfers.instructions: After the Bund lights, walk back along Nanjing Road East toward the hotel. Stop for a snack along the way.
transfers.note: Whole loop runs under a kilometer.

days.label: Day 3
days.display_title: Shanghai depth: Yu Garden, Tianzifang, Pudong skyline
days.focus: Old Shanghai gardens, French Concession lanes, then a sunset observation deck across the river.
days.transport_note: Use the metro between districts. Yu Garden and Tianzifang are short Line 10 / Line 9 hops.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Yu Garden
start_transfer.instructions: Walk south through Huangpu about 15 to 20 minutes, or take Line 10 to Yuyuan Garden Station and exit toward the bazaar.
start_transfer.note: Arrive close to opening to skip the worst of the bus tour crowd.

places.name: Yu Garden and Yuyuan Bazaar
places.summary: Ming-era classical garden surrounded by a busy bazaar of pavilions, snack stalls, and the famous Huxinting teahouse on the zigzag bridge.
highlights: Huxinting Teahouse on the zigzag bridge
Nanxiang Steamed Bun stall in the bazaar
places.transport: Metro Line 10 to Yuyuan Garden Station, Exit 1 or 3. Walk through the bazaar to the garden entrance.

foods.name: Xiaolongbao
foods.description: Steamed soup dumplings with a thin pleated wrap and a hot pork-and-broth filling. Bite the side, sip the soup, then eat.
foods.where: Nanxiang Steamed Bun Restaurant on the Yuyuan Bazaar bridge, or any branch of Din Tai Fung in Shanghai.

images.title: File:Yank Sing Shanghai Dumpling on Soup Spoon (24441687126).jpg

places.name: Tianzifang
places.summary: Maze of shikumen lane houses turned into small studios, cafes, and craft shops. The most photogenic slice of the old French Concession.
highlights: Easy walking grid in a small footprint
places.transport: Metro Line 9 to Dapuqiao Station, Exit 1. Tianzifang sits across the road.

foods.name: Cong You Ban Mian (Scallion Oil Noodles)
foods.description: Chewy noodles tossed with crispy scallions, soy, and dark vinegar. Cheap, fragrant, and very Shanghainese.
foods.where: Wei Xiang Zhai near People's Square, or any neighborhood noodle shop in the French Concession.

places.name: Shanghai Tower Observation Deck
places.summary: Second-tallest building in the world. The 118th-floor deck gives a clean sunset-to-night transition over the Bund and the Huangpu River.
highlights: Top of the world's fastest elevator
118F observation deck with full skyline view
places.transport: Metro Line 2 to Lujiazui Station, Exit 6. Follow signs to Shanghai Tower (Building 3). Book tickets online for the timed slot.
places.access_note: Pre-book a sunset slot if possible. Walk-up tickets are sold but the queue can be long on a Saturday.

transfers.instructions: From Yuyuan Garden walk to Laoximen Station, take Line 10 one stop to South Shaanxi Road, transfer to Line 9 toward Dapuqiao for Tianzifang.
transfers.note: Or grab a quick Didi if you want to save the transfer time.

transfers.instructions: From Dapuqiao take Line 9 to Century Avenue, then Line 2 one stop to Lujiazui for Shanghai Tower.
transfers.note: Aim to be at Lujiazui by 90 minutes before sunset.

days.label: Day 4
days.focus: Bullet train out of Hongqiao for a classical-garden day in Suzhou, then back to Shanghai for dinner.
days.transport_note: Buy round-trip high-speed rail (G-train) tickets in advance via Trip.com or 12306. Hongqiao to Suzhou runs about 30 minutes.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Suzhou (Hongqiao + G-train)
start_transfer.instructions: Take Metro Line 2 west to Shanghai Hongqiao Railway Station, then catch a G-train to Suzhou. From Suzhou Station, taxi or Metro Line 4 to the gardens.
start_transfer.note: Arrive Hongqiao at least 40 minutes before departure for ID check and security.

places.name: Humble Administrator's Garden
places.summary: Largest classical garden in Suzhou. UNESCO-listed pavilions, lotus ponds, and corridors that frame the water from every angle.
places.transport: Metro Line 4 to Beisita Station, then a 10 minute walk. Or taxi from Suzhou Railway Station (about 10 minutes).

places.name: Pingjiang Road
places.summary: Canal-side stone street with Ming-era houses, teahouses, and stone bridges. The most preserved corner of old Suzhou.
places.transport: Walk south from Humble Administrator's Garden, about 15 minutes. Or short taxi.

foods.name: Songshu Gui Yu (Squirrel Mandarin Fish)
foods.description: Mandarin fish scored, fried into a squirrel-tail shape, and topped with a sweet-sour glaze. The signature Suzhou banquet dish.
foods.where: Songhelou Restaurant on Taijian Lane, the original Suzhou location.
image_queries: songshu yu squirrel mandarin fish Suzhou

images.title: File:Riverside of Pingjiang Road, Gusu, Suzhou, Jiangsu, China, 215000.jpg

manual_images.title: File:Riverside of Pingjiang Road, Gusu, Suzhou, Jiangsu, China, 215000.jpg

places.name: Master of the Nets Garden
places.summary: The smallest of the famous Suzhou gardens, often the most loved. Tight courtyards designed for layered borrowed views.
places.transport: Short taxi or 20 minute walk from Pingjiang Road. Located on Kuojiatou Lane.
image_queries: Master of the Nets Garden Suzhou

images.title: File:Master of the Nets Garden Suzhou.jpg

manual_images.title: File:Master of the Nets Garden Suzhou.jpg

transfers.instructions: Head south from the garden along Lindun Road into Pingjiang Road's canal lane.
transfers.note: Stop in a teahouse if the weather is warm.

transfers.instructions: From Pingjiang, taxi southwest to Master of the Nets Garden.
transfers.note: Walking is doable but the taxi saves 20 minutes.

days.label: Day 5
days.focus: Early flight south to Zhangjiajie, then a long road transfer to the lantern-lit ancient town of Fenghuang.
days.transport_note: Pre-book a private car or shared shuttle from Zhangjiajie Hehua Airport (DYG) to Fenghuang. The drive is about 4 hours.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Pudong Airport
start_transfer.instructions: Leave the hotel by 04:30 to be at PVG by 05:30. Metro Line 2 is the most reliable pre-dawn option, but a taxi from Huangpu runs about 50 minutes at that hour.
start_transfer.note: International leg counts as same-airport transfer. Confirm Terminal 2 for the connecting Sichuan/Juneyao flight.

places.name: Fenghuang Ancient Town
places.summary: Stilted Diaojiaolou houses lean over the Tuojiang River. After dark the lanterns turn on and the entire town reflects on the water.
highlights: Hong Bridge (Hongqiao Wind and Rain Bridge)
Stilt houses along the Tuojiang River
places.transport: Drop-off from the airport transfer is at the West Gate. The hotel sits a short walk inside the old town.

foods.name: Miao Sour Fish Soup
foods.description: Whole-fish hotpot in a sour fermented broth with tomatoes, herbs, and chilies. The signature Miao minority dish around Fenghuang.
foods.where: Family-run restaurants on Laoying Shao Street and Hongqiao area in the old town.

foods.name: Fenghuang Ginger Candy
foods.description: Hand-pulled chewy ginger candy folded with peanuts. Watch the makers slap and pull it on wooden pegs along the riverside.
foods.where: Open-front candy shops along the Tuojiang River near Hong Bridge.

days.label: Day 6
days.focus: Daylight walk through the ancient town, then the road back north to Zhangjiajie before dark.
days.transport_note: Book the same driver back to Zhangjiajie or a shared shuttle for the afternoon. About 4 hours.

start_transfer.title: Homestay to Tuojiang River
start_transfer.instructions: Step out of the homestay onto Jinping Road, walk to the riverside, and follow the embankment north toward Hong Bridge.
start_transfer.note: Earlier the better. The day-trip tour buses arrive after 10:00.

places.name: Tuojiang River and Stilt Houses
places.summary: The same town in daylight is a different place. Walk the river edge for the diaojiaolou stilt houses, the laundry stones, and small bridges.
highlights: Hong Bridge from the water level
places.transport: Walking start from the homestay. The full riverside loop is about 1.5 km.

places.name: North Gate Tower and Old City Wall
places.summary: The surviving Ming-Qing gate that guarded the riverside town. Climb the wall for a high view of the rooftops and Hongqiao.
places.transport: Walk inside the old town. The North Gate sits at the river bend just north of Hong Bridge.

transfers.instructions: From the river path, head one block inland to the North Gate Tower at the bend in the wall.
transfers.note: Pick up a snack on the riverside lanes on the way back.

days.label: Day 7
days.focus: Avatar mountains. Take the elevator up, walk the rim, and let the sandstone pillars do the work.
days.transport_note: Buy the multi-day park pass at the Wulingyuan or Forest Park entrance. Save the same pass for tomorrow if Tianmen is split.

start_transfer.title: Inn to Forest Park Entrance
start_transfer.instructions: Pre-arrange a hotel taxi to the Wulingyuan or Forest Park gate. Both gates connect by free park shuttle to the same trails.
start_transfer.note: Wulingyuan side is closer to the Bailong Elevator and Yuanjiajie.

places.name: Yuanjiajie and Avatar Hallelujah Mountain
places.summary: Quartzite sandstone pillars rise from the green valley. The Hallelujah pillar is the famous Avatar movie reference.
places.transport: Take the Bailong Elevator up to the Yuanjiajie plateau, then walk the rim trail.

manual_images.title: File:Stunning rock formations in Bailong Yuanjiajie scenic area.jpg
File:Stunning rock formations from the top of Bailong, Yuanjiajie scenic area.jpg

places.name: Bailong Elevator
places.summary: Glass elevator bolted onto a sandstone cliff. 326 meters in under 90 seconds, cutting hours off the climb to Yuanjiajie.
highlights: Tallest outdoor lift in the world
places.transport: Free park shuttle from the Wulingyuan gate drops at the lower station. Buy the elevator add-on ticket separately.

places.name: Tianzi Mountain
places.summary: Northern pillar field. Cable car up to the rim for the West Sea Stone Forest and the King-of-Heaven viewpoint.
places.transport: Park shuttle from Yuanjiajie connects to the Tianzi Mountain cable car bottom station.

places.name: Golden Whip Stream
places.summary: Flat valley walk along a clear stream with pillars rising on both sides. A break from the rim trails after the elevator and cable car.
places.transport: Walk down via the trail or take the shuttle to the Forest Park entrance and follow the stream upstream.

foods.name: Tujia Sanxiaguo (Three-Layer Pot)
foods.description: Tujia clay-pot stew layered with smoked pork, root vegetables, and chilies. Heavy, smoky, and warming after a day on the trails.
foods.where: Local Tujia restaurants in Zhangjiajie city or Wulingyuan town.

transfers.instructions: Free shuttle connects Yuanjiajie, Tianzi Mountain, and Forest Park gates. Show your park pass on each ride.
transfers.note: Last shuttle leaves the rim around 18:00. Plan the descent before that.

transfers.instructions: Walk from the Tianzi cable car lower station along the valley road into Golden Whip Stream.
transfers.note: Or shuttle down if the legs are done.

transfers.instructions: Follow the stream out toward the Forest Park gate, then take the shuttle and a taxi back to the inn.
transfers.note: Save energy. Tianmen Mountain is tomorrow.

days.label: Day 8
days.display_title: Tianmen Mountain, then night flight to Beijing
days.focus: Cable-car the long ridge, walk the glass skywalk, climb to Heaven's Gate, then late flight to Beijing.
days.transport_note: Leave bags packed at the inn for late checkout. Book a 19:30 transfer to the airport for the 22:30 departure.

start_transfer.title: Inn to Tianmen Cableway
start_transfer.instructions: Taxi or Didi to the Tianmen Mountain Cableway lower station in central Zhangjiajie city. About 15 to 20 minutes from the inn.
start_transfer.note: The cableway base is right next to the train station for easy taxi drop-off.

places.name: Tianmen Mountain Cableway
places.summary: One of the longest passenger cable cars in the world. The 7.5 km ride climbs from the city straight to the cliff plateau.
highlights: Drops you on the rim trail
places.transport: Cableway lower station next to Zhangjiajie Railway Station. Buy the bundled park ticket at the gate.

places.name: Coiling Dragon Cliff Glass Skywalk
places.summary: Glass-floored walkway bolted to a vertical cliff face. About 100 meters of full-glass section over an open drop.
highlights: View down to the 99 Bends Road
Photogenic and scary in equal parts
places.transport: Walk along the rim trail clockwise from the cableway top station.
places.access_note: Shoe covers are required and rented at the entrance. Bags can be checked free.

places.name: Heaven's Gate (Tianmen Cave)
places.summary: Natural limestone arch, 131 meters tall, with 999 stairs cut up the mountain face. Take the escalators inside the cliff back down.
places.transport: Take the rim trail back to the central plaza, then ride the mountain shuttle and 7-stage escalators down to the cave.

transfers.instructions: Continue clockwise on the rim trail from the skywalk to the central plaza for the descent route.
transfers.note: Skip the second skywalk (Walk of Faith) if pressed for time.

transfers.instructions: Shuttle bus down to the upper escalator, then ride the multi-stage escalators inside the cliff to Heaven's Gate plaza.
transfers.note: Plan to be back at the inn by 18:00 to repack and head to DYG for the night flight.

days.label: Day 9
days.display_title: Beijing core: Forbidden City and Jingshan
days.focus: Slow start after the late arrival, then Tiananmen, the Forbidden City, and a Jingshan sunset.
days.transport_note: Book the Forbidden City entry online in advance via gugong.ktmtech.cn. Tickets sell out and you cannot buy at the gate.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Tiananmen East
start_transfer.instructions: From the Guomao area take Metro Line 1 west to Tiananmen East Station, Exit A.
start_transfer.note: Bring your passport. There is a security check before entering Tiananmen Square.

places.name: Tiananmen Square
places.summary: The world's largest public square. Rostrum to the north, Mao's Mausoleum and the Monument to the People's Heroes in the middle.
places.transport: Metro Line 1 to Tiananmen East. Pass the security check, then walk through the underpass.
places.access_note: Passport required for the security check. Pre-registration is recommended.

images.title: File:Beijing China Tiananmen-Square-Young Pioneers of China-01.jpg

places.name: Forbidden City (Palace Museum)
places.summary: Ming and Qing imperial palace. 980 surviving buildings on a north-south axis. Three hours covers the central halls and the back gardens.
highlights: Imperial Garden at the north end
places.transport: Enter at Meridian Gate (south), exit at Gate of Divine Prowess (north). The route is one-way.
places.access_note: Mandatory online booking the day before. Closed on Mondays.
image_queries: Palace Museum Beijing Hall of Supreme Harmony

foods.name: Peking Duck
foods.description: Lacquered roast duck carved tableside. Eat the crisp skin with sugar, then wrap the meat in thin pancakes with scallion and hoisin.
foods.where: Siji Minfu (Wangfujing branch) for a more refined plate, or Da Dong (Jinbao Place) for the modern version.

images.title: File:Peking duck by Mr Wabu in Beijing.jpg
File:The Forbidden City - View from Coal Hill.jpg

places.name: Jingshan Park
places.summary: Artificial hill made from the soil dug for the palace moat. The summit pavilion gives the classic axial sunset view of the Forbidden City roofs.
places.transport: Walk across from the north gate of the Forbidden City to Jingshan's south entrance.
image_queries: Jingshan Park Beijing sunset Forbidden City

foods.name: Zha Jiang Mian
foods.description: Hand-cut wheat noodles topped with a salty fermented soybean and pork sauce, plus julienned vegetables you toss in.
foods.where: Old Beijing Zhajiangmian Daiwang in Qianmen, or any Lao Beijing noodle house.

transfers.instructions: Walk straight north from Tiananmen through the underpass and into the Meridian Gate of the Forbidden City.
transfers.note: Tickets are checked at the gate.

transfers.instructions: From the Forbidden City's north gate, cross Jingshanqian Street into Jingshan Park's south entrance and climb to Wanchun Pavilion.
transfers.note: Aim for 30 to 45 min before sunset for the best light.

days.label: Day 10
days.display_title: Mutianyu Great Wall and 798 Art District
days.focus: Out to the wall in the morning, back into the city for an art-and-warehouse evening.
days.transport_note: Book a private car for Mutianyu round-trip (about 90 minutes each way) or use the Mubus from Dongzhimen Wai.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Mutianyu
start_transfer.instructions: Pickup at 07:30 from the hotel. Drive northeast to Huairou and the Mutianyu base. The driver waits at the lot for the return.
start_transfer.note: Alternatively, take the official Mutianyu shuttle bus from Dongzhimen Wai. Slower but cheaper.

places.name: Mutianyu Great Wall
places.summary: Restored Ming wall section in green hills. Cable car up, walk a long restored stretch, slide back down on the toboggan.
places.transport: Park shuttle from the visitor center to the cable car base. Walk uphill if you prefer the trail.
places.access_note: Far less crowded than Badaling. Total round trip from central Beijing runs 6 to 7 hours.

places.name: 798 Art District
places.summary: Bauhaus factory complex turned into galleries, studios, and bookshops. Slogans on the brick, exposed pipes, and contemporary work inside.
places.transport: Metro Line 14 to Wangjing South Station, then a 10 minute walk. Or taxi direct from the hotel.

foods.name: Old Beijing Lamb Hotpot
foods.description: Brass chimney pot of clear broth with thinly sliced mutton, daikon, and sesame paste dip. Lao Beijing winter staple, eaten year-round.
foods.where: Manfu Lou or Yang Da Ye, both classic Beijing instant-boil mutton hotpot houses.

transfers.instructions: Driver returns from Mutianyu to 798 (about 60 minutes). Or taxi from Mutianyu's lot if not pre-arranged.
transfers.note: If energy is low, head straight back to the hotel and walk Guomao or Sanlitun in the evening instead.

days.label: Day 11
days.focus: Early flight south. China leg ends and the Taiwan itinerary picks up the same morning.
days.transport_note: Pre-book an airport transfer the night before. Beijing Capital (PEK) is the right airport for HU7987.

start_transfer.title: Hotel to Beijing Capital Airport
start_transfer.instructions: Pickup by 04:30 from the hotel. Direct taxi to PEK Terminal 2 (Hainan Airlines) takes about 30 to 45 minutes at that hour.
start_transfer.note: Confirm Terminal 2 for HU7987. Allow time for the international check-in and Taiwan entry forms.
